nodes.label: Ambisonic Installation
nodes.description: The project brings together immersive audio experiences, spatial sound works, and experimental installations that expand the possibilities of listening.

nodes.label: Art To Wear
nodes.description: The project explores the body as an artistic medium through costume design, textiles, wearable art, jewelry, performance design, styling, craft-based practices, and interdisciplinary making.

nodes.label: Assembling Ritual
nodes.description: The project explores rituals through the intersections of art, science, technology, society, anthropology, philosophy, culture, and experimental research practices.

nodes.label: Communitas
nodes.description: The project explores the idea of rituals through traditional music practices, folk traditions, contemporary technologies, electronic approaches, and other forms of expression discovered along the way.

nodes.label: Contemporary Art
nodes.description: Rituals of Matter. During the concerts, microscopic rituals of matter become live visuals: dissolving crystals, spreading pigments, microorganisms, liquids, and organic particles are captured in real time and transformed through Resolume into a reactive visual environment that evolves together with the music.

nodes.label: Documental Art
nodes.description: The documentary film Notations #2 will capture the inner life of the laboratory, following its production process from the initial creative sparks to the final showcase. Instead of a traditional format, the narrative places a sharp focus on the unique personal stories of the participants, crew, and curators.

nodes.label: Installation
nodes.description: The project presents multimedia installations, audiovisual performances, and spatial interventions that transform the festival into a site of experimentation, interaction, and discovery.

nodes.label: Music Production
nodes.description: The program showcases artists working across music, sound, technology, and performance, highlighting new approaches to listening, composition, and collective creation.

nodes.label: Neo-Tribes
nodes.description: The project explores the idea of rituals through jazz language, improvisation, groove, sound experimentation, electronic music, and other forms of expression discovered along the way.

nodes.label: Reverse Tower Ritual
nodes.description: Reverse Tower is a ritual in which human signals become light and sound, creating an experience of cosmic perspective and temporarily dissolving the borders between people.

nodes.label: Sama Soundbath
nodes.description: A sacral listening space — a sound environment where music is experienced through silence, full-body immersion, and shared presence. The space is built around a high-quality sound system designed to be felt as much as heard: sound travels through the room and through the body.

nodes.label: Set Design
nodes.description: Our set design team will be with us throughout the lab, helping transform each venue into a festival space. Feel free to reach out to them if you need help with your project, installation, or spatial setup.

nodes.label: Somatic Practices
nodes.description: The project explores embodiment, ritual, presence, and collective practices through performance, dance, choreography, movement research, theater, sound-and-body practices, and interdisciplinary artistic approaches.

nodes.label: The Refrain
nodes.description: The project explores the idea of rituals through classical instruments, composition, conducting, electro-acoustic music, experimental performance, and other forms of expression discovered along the way.

nodes.label: Video Art
nodes.description: The project brings together artists working with moving image, film, animation, archives, and audiovisual storytelling, presenting new visual works and experiments throughout the festival.

nodes.label: Zine Art
nodes.description: A unique zine will be produced during the laboratory: if you love working with texts, taking interviews, translating, researching or if you have a nice essay or article written about rituals, folk culture, collaborative practices — we might find a role for you!
